description: os-use desktop computer-use benchmark (hermes-desktop). Measures agent capability across several cases: (1) connect remotely via SSH (the agent fills the SSH form with real OS input and hermes opens a real SSH tunnel to enter the main UI), (2) navigate to the Settings screen after connecting. A VLM judge scores each case's final screen → a scorecard (per-case pass + aggregate pass-rate) for a fair comparison across harnesses. Build the image with scripts/live/Dockerfile.hermes-ssh-agent (hermes + sshd + /agent.cjs).

cases.task: Connect Hermes to the SSH server at 127.0.0.1 as user root (key /root/.ssh/id_rsa).

config.rubric: PASS only if the app has advanced PAST the SSH connection form — e.g. a splash like 'Starting SSH tunnel…' or the main app screen (sidebar with Chat/Discover, an 'Ask anything' box) — with NO connection-error message. The SSH connection form itself or a red connection error is NOT the goal.

cases.task: After connecting, open the application Settings screen and show its configuration options.

config.rubric: PASS only if the Hermes Settings/preferences page is OPEN — the main content area shows settings/configuration controls. The Chat screen, the SSH/remote connection forms, or the welcome screen are NOT the goal (a 'Settings' link in the sidebar alone is NOT sufficient).
